slides.header: How to Use the Fetal Kick Count Chart
body: The best time to count kicks is whenever your baby is typically the most active. Ask the client if she feels the baby moving around a lot at a certain time each day? If the baby doesn’t have a predictable pattern, after dinner is a good time to try.

slides.header: How to Use the Fetal Kick Count Chart (continued)
slides.intro: To fill out the card, follow these steps:

layers.text: Lie down on her side or relax in a comfortable chair.
Make a note of the time.
Pay attention only to her baby’s movements. Count any movement that she can feel (except hiccups). Any twist, kick, or turn is 1 movement.

After you count 10 movements, check the time and record on the card how many minutes it took. If the baby does not kick or move within 1 hour, advise her to do some or all of the following things and then try again
Eat or drink something, like fruit or juice.

body: For Community Health Workers (CHWs), recognizing urgent maternal warning signs during pregnancy is a critical skill that can help prevent serious complications and maternal mortality. Training on these signs should cover physical and mental health indicators, enabling CHWs to know when to escalate a client's concerns to a healthcare provider.
Be aware of urgent maternal warning signs and symptoms during pregnancy and in the year after delivery.
Seek medical care immediately if you experience any signs or symptoms that are listed.
